diagrams.title: Critical Section Structure
diagrams.description: Shows the four sections of code execution for a process in the Critical Section problem context
diagrams.purpose: Helps understand the synchronization flow and where each requirement applies

diagrams.title: Peterson's Algorithm Flow
diagrams.description: Two-process mutual exclusion algorithm showing how flag and turn variables coordinate entry
elements: Process 0 Entry: flag[0]=true; turn=1; while(flag[1] && turn==1)
Process 1 Entry: flag[1]=true; turn=0; while(flag[0] && turn==0)
Both set flags to false on exit
diagrams.purpose: Visualizes the coordination between two processes using shared variables

diagrams.title: Race Condition Example
diagrams.description: Timeline showing how two processes accessing shared variable without synchronization leads to incorrect results
elements: Final value depends on timing - both outcomes possible
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates why critical section solutions are necessary
